pullRequest.title: fix: guard against None model_name in OpenAI endpoint (#1700)
pullRequest.body: Fix AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'lower' that occurs when model_name is None (common with Azure OpenAI deployments). The issue manifests when a None model_name propagates to langchain's standardize_model_name() which calls .lower() on it without null-checking. Addresses https://github.com/truera/trulens/issues/1700 Changes: - handle_wrapped_call: Check bindings.arguments as fallback, fall back to response.model when model is None - _handle_response: Guard model_name to ensure it is always a string - handle_generation_chunk: Guard response.model with 'or empty string' - OpenAICostComputer.handle_response: Guard first_chunk.model Add 11 unit tests covering both non-OTEL and OTEL code paths. # Description Please include a summary of the changes and the related issue that can be included in the release announcement. Please also include relevant motivation and context. ## Other details good to know for developers Please include any other details of this change useful for _TruLens_ developers. ## Type of change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] New Tests - [ ] This change includes re-generated golden test results - [ ] This change requires a documentation update ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fix `AttributeError` in OpenAI endpoint by ensuring `model_name` is always a string, with fallbacks for `None` values, and add tests to verify. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fix `AttributeError` in `handle_wrapped_call`, `_handle_response`, `handle_generation_chunk`, and `OpenAICostComputer.handle_response` by ensuring `model_name` is always a string. > - Fallback to `response.model` or default to an empty string if `model_name` is `None`. > - **Testing**: > - Add 11 unit tests in `test_none_model_name.py` to cover scenarios with `None` `model_name` in both OTEL and non-OTEL code paths. > - Tests ensure no crashes occur and correct fallbacks are used for `model_name`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=truera%2Ftrulens&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for e36a664b74c95429a8e01c7a0f75b77f432d1283. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/truera/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

pullRequest.title: Fix: Skip instrumentation of non-callable objects (e.g., CallTypes enum)
pullRequest.body: ## Description When creating a `LiteLLM` provider, the endpoint instrumentation fails with an `AttributeError` because it tries to access `func.__name__` on a `CallTypes` enum instead of a callable function. **Error:** ``` File "trulens/core/feedback/endpoint.py", line 857, in wrap_function logger.debug("Instrumenting %s for %s.", func.__name__, self.name) AttributeError: 'CallTypes' object has no attribute '__name__' ``` ## Root Cause The `_instrument_module_members` method iterates through all classes in a module (e.g., `litellm`) and calls `_instrument_class` to instrument methods like `completion`. However, `litellm.CallTypes` is an enum class where `CallTypes.completion` is an enum member, not a callable function. When `wrap_function` receives this enum value, it fails trying to access `__name__`. ## Fix Added a check at the beginning of `wrap_function` to skip non-callable objects: ```python if not callable(func): logger.debug("Skipping instrumentation of non-callable %s", func) return func ``` This allows instrumentation to continue for actual functions while gracefully skipping non-callable objects like enum values. ## Testing Verified the fix resolves the issue when creating a LiteLLM provider: ```python from trulens.providers.litellm import LiteLLM provider = LiteLLM(model_engine="openai/gpt-4") # Now works ``` ## Related - Fixes #2327 - Context: MLflow TruLens integration PR https://github.com/mlflow/mlflow/pull/19492 ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Add check in `wrap_function` to skip non-callable objects, fixing `AttributeError` in `endpoint.py`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Add check in `wrap_function` in `endpoint.py` to skip non-callable objects, preventing `AttributeError` when accessing `__name__` on non-callables. > - **Testing**: > - Verified fix with `LiteLLM` provider instantiation, ensuring no errors occur. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=truera%2Ftrulens&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 914a4c53ed2d66615f86db8a8b272e5ca3b7e0fd. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/truera/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

pullRequest.title: Drop pkg_resources dependency from trulens-core
pullRequest.body: ## Summary Removes the `pkg_resources` dependency from trulens-core. `pkg_resources` (from setuptools) is deprecated and not available by default in modern Python environments managed by `uv`, causing import failures at runtime. Closes #2350 ## Changes **`src/core/pyproject.toml`** - Bumped `munch` from `^2.5.0` to `>=3.0.0,<5.0.0`. munch 2.5.0 imports `pkg_resources` at module scope; munch 3.x dropped that dependency. **`src/core/trulens/core/utils/imports.py`** - Removed the `import pkg_resources` statement. - Replaced `pkg_resources.parse_requirements(fh)` in `_requirements_of_trulens_core_file()` with `packaging.requirements.Requirement`, which was already imported and available as a dependency (`packaging >= 23.0`). ## Root cause Two separate code paths pulled in `pkg_resources`: 1. **Transitive**: `munch` 2.5.0 imported `pkg_resources` at the module level. In `uv`-managed environments where `setuptools` is not bundled, this caused `ModuleNotFoundError` on any `import trulens`. 2. **Direct**: `_requirements_of_trulens_core_file()` called `pkg_resources.parse_requirements()` to parse internal requirements files. This is now handled by `packaging.requirements.Requirement`. ## Testing ``` $ python3 -m pytest tests/unit/core/ -v 33 passed $ python3 -c "from packaging import requirements; req = requirements.Requirement('trulens-core >= 1.0.0'); print(req.name)" trulens-core $ python3 -c "import munch; print(munch.__version__); m = munch.Munch({'a': 1}); print(m.a)" 4.0.0 1 ``` cc @sfc-gh-jreini ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Remove deprecated `pkg_resources` dependency from `trulens-core` by updating `munch` and using `packaging.requirements.Requirement`. > > - **Dependencies**: > - Removed `pkg_resources` dependency from `trulens-core`. > - Updated `munch` version in `pyproject.toml` to `>=3.0.0,<5.0.0` to eliminate `pkg_resources` import. > - **Code Changes**: > - Removed `import pkg_resources` from `imports.py`. > - Replaced `pkg_resources.parse_requirements()` with `packaging.requirements.Requirement` in `_requirements_of_trulens_core_file()` in `imports.py`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=truera%2Ftrulens&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ace19d2885d46b15a99e870aa286cf1b8922ef36. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/truera/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

pullRequest.title: Dashboard Performance: SQL-level aggregation for dashboard leaderboard
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes the dashboard leaderboard being extremely slow when record count exceeds 10k (20-30s load times, 1-2 min for "Show all"). The root cause was that the leaderboard fetched **all individual records** with full JSON payloads, deserialized them in Python, then aggregated with pandas `groupby` — when it only needs per-app-version aggregates.

### Changes

- **SQL-level aggregation** (`get_leaderboard_aggregates`): Pushes `GROUP BY` into SQL via SQLAlchemy's cross-dialect query builder. Two-step approach: (1) base metrics from `RECORD_ROOT` spans, (2) feedback averages from `EVAL_ROOT` spans, with a small Python pivot on the already-aggregated result. Works across SQLite and PostgreSQL.
- **Sort order fix**: Changed OTEL record pagination from `ASC` to `DESC` so newest records appear first.
- **DB indexes**: Added Alembic migration and ORM declarations for `start_timestamp` and `timestamp` indexes on the event table.
- **Leaderboard rewiring**: The leaderboard tab now uses the aggregation path; histogram tab lazy-loads raw records only when selected.
- **EVAL_ROOT metric name fix**: Fixed `_get_records_and_feedback_otel` to also check `SpanAttributes.EVAL_ROOT.METRIC_NAME` (not just `EVAL.METRIC_NAME`) when parsing feedback spans.
- **Seed & benchmark scripts**: Added `scripts/seed_dashboard_test.py` (10k OTEL records) and `scripts/benchmark_dashboard.py` for reproducible testing.

### Benchmark Results (10k records, SQLite, 5 runs each)

| Scenario | Old (s) | New (s) | Speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| Single app, 3 versions | 0.309 | 0.114 | **2.7x** |
| Single app, limit=1000 | 0.194 | 0.118 | **1.6x** |
| All apps, 15 versions | 1.330 | 0.255 | **5.2x** |
| Single version | 0.163 | 0.090 | **1.8x** |

### Files Changed

- `src/core/trulens/core/database/sqlalchemy.py` — New aggregation methods + sort fix
- `src/core/trulens/core/database/base.py` — Abstract method + EVAL_ROOT metric name fix
- `src/core/trulens/core/database/orm.py` — Index declarations on Event table
- `src/core/trulens/core/database/migrations/versions/11_add_event_table_indexes.py` — Alembic migration
- `src/dashboard/trulens/dashboard/tabs/Leaderboard.py` — Rewired to use aggregation path
- `src/dashboard/trulens/dashboard/utils/dashboard_utils.py` — Cached wrapper for aggregation
- `scripts/seed_dashboard_test.py` — 10k OTEL record seed script
- `scripts/benchmark_dashboard.py` — Benchmark script
- `scripts/launch_dashboard.sh` — Side-by-side dashboard launcher

## Related Issues

- Fixes #2372 — Dashboard leaderboard extremely slow with >10k records
- Related to #2303 — Dashboard performance improvements
- Related to #1495 — Slow dashboard loading times
- Related to #1162 — Dashboard query optimization

## Test plan

- [x] Benchmark script confirms 1.6-5.2x speedup across all scenarios
- [x] Leaderboard loads correctly with aggregated data (pinned apps, cost columns, feedback scores)
- [x] Compare page works with OTEL records (EVAL_ROOT metric name fix)
- [x] Histogram tab lazy-loads raw records only when selected
- [x] Lint and format pass (ruff)
- [ ] Unit tests (`make test-unit`)

.... Generated with [Cortex Code](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cortex-code/cortex-code)
